https://kenyalaw.org/caselaw/cases/view/: Republic v Job Kipkorir Arap Chumba [2023] eKLR. Application for pretrial release of the accused person charged with murder contrary to Section 203 as read with Section 204 of the Penal Code. The court considered bail conditions for capital offences and the seriousness of the charge.

Safaricom PLC v Commissioner of Domestic Taxes [2023] eKLR. Tax dispute concerning withholding tax on mobile money transfer services. The tribunal examined the definition of passive income under Section 35(3) of the Income Tax Act.

Okiya Omtatah Okoiti v Attorney General [2023] eKLR. Public interest litigation challenging the constitutionality of the Finance Act 2023. The petitioner argued that the amendments violated Article 201 principles of public finance.

Kenya National Commission on Human Rights v Attorney General [2017] eKLR. Advisory opinion on the rights of suspects in police custody. The Supreme Court held that police detention beyond 24 hours without court appearance is unconstitutional.

Centre for Rights Education and Awareness v Attorney General [2017] eKLR. Constitutional petition challenging discriminatory provisions in the Succession Act affecting widows and female children in intestate succession.

British American Tobacco v Cabinet Secretary for the National Treasury [2019] eKLR. Challenge to plain packaging requirements under the Public Health Act. The court upheld the public health justification.

Federation of Women Lawyers Kenya v Attorney General [2014] eKLR. Challenge to provisions of the Marriage Act permitting child marriage with parental consent. The court declared the provision unconstitutional.

Katiba Institute v Attorney General [2022] eKLR. Challenge to the appointment of Principal Secretaries without public participation. The court emphasized the importance of public participation under Article 10.
Mathew Mwangi Mutiso v IEBC [2023] eKLR. Election petition challenging gubernatorial election results in Kiambu County for alleged irregularities and voter suppression.
Doctors for Life International v Speaker of the National Assembly [2006] ZACC 11. Landmark case on separation of powers and the role of the judiciary in reviewing legislative and executive action.

Standard Chartered Bank v Kenya Revenue Authority [2022] eKLR. Dispute over corporation tax on foreign-sourced income and the application of double taxation agreements between Kenya and other jurisdictions.
Wiper Democratic Movement v Jubilee Party [2022] eKLR. Dispute arising from a coalition agreement and the allocation of party tickets for the 2022 general elections.
Republic v Chief Magistrate's Court at Milimani [2023] eKLR. Judicial review seeking certiorari to quash a decision in a land dispute. The court examined the limits of judicial review jurisdiction.
Nairobi City County v Attorney General [2021] eKLR. Constitutional dispute over the division of revenue between the national government and Nairobi City County Government under Article 202.

Kenya Railways Corporation v Federation of Kenya Employers [2020] eKLR. Labour dispute on retrenchment of employees during restructuring. The court applied Section 40 of the Employment Act on termination.
Societe Internationale de Telecommunications Aeronautiques v Kenya Revenue Authority [2021] eKLR. Whether aeronautical telecommunications services constitute a taxable supply under the VAT Act.
Mombasa Cement Ltd v County Government of Mombasa [2022] eKLR. Challenge to county government levies on industrial operations. The court examined the constitutional limits of county taxation powers.
Oxygen House Limited v Attorney General [2023] eKLR. Constitutional petition challenging EMCA provisions on waste disposal. The court considered environmental protection obligations.
Maurice Onyango Oluoch v Nairobi City Water & Sewerage Company [2019] eKLR. Class action for chronic water shortages and illegal disconnections.
Communications Authority of Kenya v Kenya Human Rights Commission [2020] eKLR. Dispute over government surveillance powers under the National Intelligence Service Act and the right to privacy.
Kenya Airports Authority v KAA Workers Union [2021] eKLR. Labour dispute arising from the privatization of JKIA operations and its effect on employee terms.
